hints.text: Something is hidden in plain sight on one of the public pages.
View the HTML source of the Products page (Ctrl+U).
Web servers often tell crawlers which paths to avoid. Have you checked?
Check /robots.txt — then follow the disallowed path.
The staff resources directory contains an onboarding document with default credentials.
After login, one page lets you search for staff. Does it sanitise input?
The lookup page builds a SQL LIKE query directly from the search field — no sanitisation.
Try a UNION SELECT to dump the users table: ' UNION SELECT username,password,3,4,5 FROM users-- -
The users table contains credentials for other accounts, not just employees.
The password column is unsalted MD5. Crack it with rockyou.txt.
The admin panel has a file management section. Does it validate what you upload?
The upload feature accepts any file type. A PHP script will execute on the server.
Uploaded files are served from /uploads/. A PHP webshell with ?cmd=cat+/flag.txt will read the flag.
